message:
refactor(demo_showcase): remove terminal_width tracking in favor of PTY cap

Now that apply_pty_width_cap() sets the PTY width at startup, the app no
longer needs to track a separate terminal_width vs layout width:

app.rs:
- Remove terminal_width field from App (was only used by pad_to_terminal_width)
- Remove pad_to_terminal_width() method entirely — with PTY capped to
  match layout width, the terminal driver handles line wrapping correctly
  and no per-line space-padding is needed
- Simplify WindowSizeMsg handler: collapse the three-branch match into
  a two-branch using actual_width.min(AUTO_MAX_WIDTH) for the None case
- Simplify overlay rendering (command palette, guided tour, notes modal):
  return view directly instead of routing through pad_to_terminal_width
- Final view() path now ends at pad_lines_to_width() without the extra
  terminal-width padding pass

main.rs:
- Extract AUTO_MAX_WIDTH as a module-level const (200) with doc comment
  noting it must match the App::update constant
- Use the const in apply_pty_width_cap() instead of magic number
- Remove redundant comments and unnecessary `as u16` cast (max_width
  is already u16 from the config)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
